title: Migrate email-intelligence + pipeline-manager READ paths from Superhuman MCP to gog Gmail
description:
Skill audit on 2026-04-26 identified 2 skills that READ from Superhuman MCP for inbox/draft scanning. Drafting via superhuman-draft.sh stays as-is (intentional). 

Scope:
- email-intelligence (.claude/skills/email-intelligence/SKILL.md): replace superhuman_search calls for draft enumeration with gog gmail search 'label:DRAFT' / 'label:SENT' + age detection
- pipeline-manager (.claude/skills/pipeline-manager/SKILL.md, lines 362-580): same pattern — replace superhuman_search with gog Gmail folder queries for sent/draft status to drive Attio stage updates

Rationale: removes Superhuman as a runtime dependency for inbox scanning. Drafting (compose) stays via Superhuman wrapper because that's intentional per security model.

Effort: ~2-3 hours combined.

title: Slim weekly-tracker to vault-only
description: Phase A complete: dashboard now renders panels for outreach drafts/sends, Kay LinkedIn DMs, new contacts (Attio count), per-niche outreach breakdown, Apollo credits placeholder. Phase B-F pending. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/linear-wiggling-toast.md

title: Launchd wrapper hardening — full coverage
description: Wrapper-level POST_RUN_CHECK validator + headless prompt + SKILL.md MANDATORY validator section for all mutating scheduled skills. Pattern: feedback_mutating_skill_hardening_pattern.md. L1+L2+L3 shipped 2026-04-25 for target-discovery Phase 2. Plist timing fix 2026-04-26: Phase 2 moved 22:00 → 15:00 so pool exists before jj-operations 18:00 fire. L4 = extend pattern to 4 remaining mutating skills.
notes:
All 4 L4 sub-beads shipped 2026-04-26 morning:
- ai-ops-jrj.1: jj-operations-sunday
- ai-ops-jrj.2: nightly-tracker-audit
- ai-ops-jrj.3: weekly-tracker
- ai-ops-jrj.4: relationship-manager
Plus Phase 2 timing fix (22:00 → 15:00) so pool exists before 18:00 jj-operations prep.
Pattern is now uniformly applied to all 5 mutating scheduled skills (target-discovery Phase 2 + 4 L4 skills).

title: Tech-stack audit: monthly vs annual across all SaaS subscriptions
description: Audit all current SaaS subscriptions in budget tracker. For each: identify billing cadence (monthly/annual), renewal date, monthly-equivalent cost, and whether tool is actively used daily. Flag annual subscriptions that should have been monthly per memory/feedback_saas_monthly_first.md. Output: ranked list of tools to (a) cancel at next renewal, (b) downgrade to monthly, (c) keep annual. Trigger: queue into next budget-manager run.
